events.summary: Restarted Faith in Mind as a fresh local-only edition package.
events.details: The earlier premature OpenZen Faith in Mind scaffold was removed before production work continued.

events.summary: Locked the free witness hunt after two consecutive no-new-free recon waves.
events.details: The broader free witness search remained open until the lock rule was satisfied, then the candidate set was frozen for production work.

events.summary: Assigned and froze stable witness sigla.
events.details: Sigla T1-T5, A1-A3, C1-C17, and S1-S5 became the canonical short witness identifiers for the edition package.

events.summary: Locked T1 as the starting copy-text spine.
events.details: T1 is the starting copy-text for the critical edition, but the lock is reversible only through explicit evidence, a decision record, and a timeline event.

events.summary: Generated stable page images and page map for T1.
events.details: The canonical T1 PDF was re-verified and rendered to 83 PNG page images at 200 DPI. A page map and OCR metadata record were created.

events.summary: Installed the first OCR engine stack for the edition package.
events.details: RapidOCR, ONNX Runtime, and OpenCV were installed because the machine did not have system Tesseract or ImageMagick available.

events.summary: Completed RapidOCR pass 1 across all T1 page images.
events.details: RapidOCR processed all 83 pages, extracted text from 80, and returned no text on the final 3 pages.

events.summary: Pinned the Python 3.12 Paddle stack to a documented compatibility pair.
events.details: The package switched to paddleocr 3.2.0, paddlepaddle 3.1.1, and paddlex 3.2.1 after the newer stack failed during prediction.

events.summary: Made PaddleOCR calibration-usable on T1-p001 with PP-OCRv4.
events.details: The default PP-OCRv5 path still crashed on this machine, but PP-OCRv4 succeeded on the calibration page with CPU, no-HPI, and no-MKL-DNN settings.

events.summary: Completed the four-engine same-page calibration slice on T1-p001.
events.details: Saved comparison outputs now exist for RapidOCR, Tesseract, EasyOCR, and PaddleOCR on the same calibration page.

events.summary: Built the first normalized T1 working spine from RapidOCR outputs.
events.details: The normalized file preserves page markers and stable line identifiers without applying editorial correction.

events.summary: Completed PaddleOCR PP-OCRv4 full pass across all T1 page images.
events.details: A resumable Python 3.12 PaddleOCR PP-OCRv4 run finished successfully across all 83 T1 pages and preserved page-level outputs.

events.summary: Started Stage 2D manual correction and opened the corrected T1 working spine.
events.details: The package now includes a corrected working text, correction log, unresolved-loci register, and a first bounded set of title and lemma-line OCR certainty fixes through T1-p079.l01.

events.summary: Recovered a broad high-confidence lemma-line spine in the corrected T1 working text.
events.details: The corrected working file now carries OCR-first recovery of title lines and poem lemma lines through T1-p079.l01, while commentary prose remains largely unresolved.

events.summary: Extended OCR support for the early noisy pages before prose correction.
events.details: Derived Paddle text companions from saved PP-OCRv4 JSON outputs and added a focused Tesseract support batch on T1-p005 to T1-p010 so early prefatory and commentary prose can be judged against multiple OCR witnesses.

events.summary: Added a full Tesseract support pass across all T1 pages.
events.details: A first full-pass attempt failed because the system Tesseract install did not resolve chi_tra traineddata. The rerun pointed TESSDATA_PREFIX at the witness-local tessdata directory and completed a saved full pass across all 83 T1 pages.

events.summary: Added geometry-sorted Paddle support text for the full T1 witness.
events.details: Saved Paddle PP-OCRv4 JSON detections were re-ordered by x-center descending and y-center ascending to create a vertical-page support view across all 83 T1 pages. This derivative is support-only and exists to reduce reading-order noise before prose correction.

events.summary: Recovered one more secure late-band poem line in the corrected T1 working text.
events.details: T1-p060.l01 was corrected to `究竟窮極不存軌則` after the broader OCR support stack made the line recoverable. The change was accepted from a strong Paddle near-match plus immediate poem-sequence context, while surrounding commentary prose remained unresolved.

state_effect.reading_before: 有什腐朝剧棉地煎茶把针树随台著衣

events.summary: Recovered one more secure poem line in the corrected T1 working text.
events.details: T1-p030.l01 was corrected to `二見不住慎勿追尋` after the broader OCR support stack made the line recoverable. The change was accepted from a strong Paddle near-match plus RapidOCR shape support and poem-sequence placement.

state_effect.reading_before: 不见特何不见吾石见之度又作康生銀子

events.summary: Recovered another secure poem line in the corrected T1 working text.
events.details: T1-p022.l01 was corrected to `遣有沒有從空背空` after RapidOCR and extracted Paddle preserved the line's core shape strongly enough for recovery within the already secured poem sequence.

events.summary: Recovered another secure poem line in the corrected T1 working text.
events.details: T1-p025.l01 was corrected to `絕言絕慮無處不通` after RapidOCR preserved the line's skeleton clearly enough and the already secured poem sequence fixed the remaining graphs.

events.summary: Recovered another secure poem line in the corrected T1 working text.
events.details: T1-p028.l01 was corrected to `前空轉變皆由妄見` after extracted Paddle preserved a near-match and the broader poem sequence fixed the remaining OCR damage.

events.summary: Recovered another secure poem line in the corrected T1 working text.
events.details: T1-p027.l01 was corrected to `須臾返照勝卻前空` after RapidOCR and extracted Paddle both preserved the core `...照...卻前空` shape strongly enough for the already secured poem sequence to fix the damaged opening graphs.

events.summary: Recovered another secure poem line in the corrected T1 working text.
events.details: T1-p063.l01 was corrected to `一切不留無可記憶` after the Paddle column-ordered support preserved `切不留...可記憶` clearly enough and the already secured sequence between `T1-p062.l01` and `T1-p064.l01` identified the full lemma.

state_effect.reading_before: 下命根药去然日维在勤用之中自然常止

events.summary: Recovered another secure poem line in the corrected T1 working text.
events.details: T1-p077.l01 was corrected to `但能如是何慮不畢` after RapidOCR and extracted Paddle both preserved the core `但能如是何...不...` shape strongly enough for the already secured closing sequence before `T1-p078.l01` to fix the damaged graphs.

events.summary: Ran focused alternate-Tesseract closing probes on the unresolved `p076/p077` band.
events.details: Additional `chi_tra` Tesseract probes with alternate page-segmentation settings were saved under `ocr/T1/ocr/tesseract-closing-probes/`, but they remained too noisy to resolve `T1-p076.l01` honestly, so no visible text change was made from this probe.

events.summary: Corrected the visually inspected `T1-p076.l01` closing-band lemma.
events.details: Direct inspection of `page-images/T1-p076.png` showed the visible lemma as `若如是必不須守`, correcting OCR damage in the current working text.

events.summary: Corrected the visually inspected `T1-p077.l01` after reversing an earlier OCR-only misassignment.
events.details: Direct inspection of `page-images/T1-p077.png` showed the opening lemma as `一即一切一切即一`, so the earlier OCR-only correction to `但能如是何慮不畢` was replaced with the visibly correct reading.

events.summary: Opened `T4` as the first comparison-control OCR package.
events.details: The Kyoto `四部録` witness `T4` was opened in-package with initial OCR metadata and page-map scaffolding so the next pass can begin page-role review and OCR without staying blocked inside `T1` commentary prose.

events.summary: Declared the bounded `T4` comparison-control preparation slice.
events.details: Before new `T4` work began, the package declared the bounded slice `comparison_control_slice_T4_page_roles_rapidocr_pass1` to cover `T4` page-role classification, a first RapidOCR pass, and synchronized updates to the process-state files.

events.summary: Completed the `T4` page-role classification pass.
events.details: The page-role pass isolates `T4-p002` to `T4-p004` as the target `Faith in Mind` body, marks `T4-p001` and `T4-p028` as wrapper/title matter, treats `T4-p027` as colophon-like tail matter, and marks `T4-p005` to `T4-p026` as non-target anthology material.

events.summary: Completed RapidOCR pass 1 across all `T4` page images.
events.details: RapidOCR processed all 28 Kyoto image pages, extracted text on 25, and returned no text on `T4-p008`, `T4-p027`, and `T4-p028`. The first comparison-control witness now has a saved OCR corpus on disk.

events.summary: Declared the bounded `T4` vs `T1` comparison slice.
events.details: Before locus-level comparison began, the package declared the bounded slice `comparison_slice_T4_body_vs_T1_lemma_spine` to compare the isolated `T4-p002` to `T4-p004` body span against the current `T1` recovered lemma spine, build the first comparison table, and refine unresolved loci only where `T4` gives clear support.

events.summary: Built the first `T4` vs `T1` comparison table.
events.details: The first comparison table records broad `T4` support for the recovered `T1` lemma spine while making the comparison-supported open problems explicit instead of silently rewriting `T1`.

events.summary: Opened the first `T4`-supported comparison problem loci explicitly.
events.details: The unresolved register now explicitly names the `T1-p021/p022` gap, the `T1-p032` sequence problem, `T1-p047.l01`, `T1-p065.l01`, and the closing-sequence issue around `T1-p075` to `T1-p079` as comparison-supported open problems.

events.summary: Applied strict four-engine compliance before stronger scan-witness editorial use.
events.details: The active workflow now treats a new East Asian scan witness as editorially provisional until its four-engine status block is recorded. Existing `T4` comparison notes remain provisional until the missing engine statuses are logged.

events.summary: Completed full `tesseract` pass on `T4` with warnings logged.
events.details: A full `T4` Tesseract pass is now saved under `ocr/T4/ocr/tesseract-full-pass/`. The run produced `.txt` outputs for all 28 pages and also emitted six scale warnings on very small fragments, which were preserved in the run summary instead of being hidden.

events.summary: Completed `EasyOCR` calibration on `T4-p002`.
events.details: The engine itself ran on `T4-p002`, but the first save attempt failed because the raw result payload included NumPy scalar values that were not JSON-serializable. The normalized rerun completed and saved a calibration slice under `ocr/T4/ocr/easyocr-calibration/`.

events.summary: Completed full `PaddleOCR PP-OCRv4` pass on `T4` and derived usable text support.
events.details: The saved `T4` Paddle pass now covers all 28 pages under `ocr/T4/ocr/paddleocr-ppocrv4/`. As on `T1`, the raw Paddle `.txt` companions are not the reliable text layer on this machine, so recoverable text was derived from `res.rec_texts` into `extracted-text/`.

events.summary: Recorded the full `T4` four-engine status block.
events.details: The package now records `T4` as `RapidOCR` full pass, `tesseract` full pass with warnings, `PaddleOCR PP-OCRv4` full pass, and `EasyOCR` calibration-only. This satisfies the requirement to record all four engine statuses, while still keeping stronger editorial reliance provisional until `EasyOCR` is extended.

events.summary: Completed full `EasyOCR` pass on `T4`.
events.details: The full `EasyOCR` pass was resumed after an earlier timeout and now covers all 28 `T4` pages under `ocr/T4/ocr/easyocr-full-pass/`. `27` pages contain extracted text and only `T4-p028` is empty.

events.summary: Cleared `T4` out of provisional engine-status state.
events.details: With the `EasyOCR` full pass saved, `T4` now has full-pass OCR coverage from `RapidOCR`, `tesseract`, `PaddleOCR PP-OCRv4`, and `EasyOCR`. The witness is no longer provisional merely because of missing engine-status work.

events.summary: Corrected `T1-p047.l01` and `T1-p065.l01` from the fully recorded `T4` comparison witness.
events.details: The first post-compliance comparison-informed correction slice changed `T1-p047.l01` to `法無異法妄自愛著` and `T1-p065.l01` to `非思量處識情難測`. Both loci had already been logged as strong `T4` repair candidates and were accepted only after `T4` reached full-pass four-engine OCR status.

events.summary: Supplied the missing line between `T1-p021.l01` and `T1-p022.l01` as stable inserted locus `T1-p021.l01a`.
events.details: After `T4` reached full-pass four-engine OCR status, the previously open comparison-supported gap between `唯滯兩邊寧知一種` and `遣有沒有從空背空` was supplied in the working text as `T1-p021.l01a` with reading `一種不通兩處失功`. The insertion was recorded explicitly rather than by silently renumbering downstream loci.

events.summary: Repaired the `T1-p031/T1-p032` poem sequence from the fully recorded `T4` comparison witness.
events.details: The mid-band sequence issue was resolved by correcting `T1-p031.l03` to `二由一有一亦莫守` and `T1-p032.l01` to `一心不生萬法無咎`, restoring the sequence immediately before the already corrected `無咎無法不生不心`.

events.summary: Supplied the missing closing locus after `T1-p077.l01` as `T1-p077.l01a`.
events.details: The current `T1` close lacked a separate corresponding locus for `但能如是何慮不畢`, which `T4-p004` preserves between `一即一切一切即一` and `信心不二不二信心`. The working text now records that line as stable inserted locus `T1-p077.l01a` while leaving the earlier `T1-p075/T1-p076` branch tension open.

events.summary: Rolled back the false poem certainty at `T1-p075.l01` after direct image review.
events.details: The earlier correction of `T1-p075.l01` to `若不如此必不須守` was removed after direct review of `page-images/T1-p075.png` and the saved `T1` OCR support showed commentary material at that locus instead of a secure closing lemma. The closing-band problem is now framed as omission-versus-substitution before `T1-p076.l01 = 若如是必不須守`.

events.summary: Closed the remaining `T1-p075/T1-p076` closing-band issue as omission rather than substitution.
events.details: The package now records that `T1-p075` remains commentary material while `T1-p076.l01 = è‹¥å¦‚æ˜¯å¿…ä¸é ˆå®ˆ` is visually secure. `T4` supports the broader transmitted close, but there is no positive `T1` evidence for restoring `è‹¥ä¸å¦‚æ­¤å¿…ä¸é ˆå®ˆ` on the previous page, so the branch question is resolved as omission in this witness.

events.summary: Opened `T5` as the second comparison-control OCR package and classified its page roles.
events.details: Created in-package `ocr/T5/metadata.json` and `ocr/T5/page-map.csv` from the Kyoto `RB00012929` image set. The target `Faith in Mind` span is confined to `T5-p004` through `T5-p006`, with wrapper material on `T5-p001` through `T5-p003`, non-target anthology material on `T5-p007` through `T5-p028`, and rear wrapper matter on `T5-p029`.

events.summary: Completed the full `RapidOCR` pass on `T5`.
events.details: Ran `RapidOCR` across all 29 `T5` source images and saved page-level `.json` and `.txt` outputs under `ocr/T5/ocr/rapidocr/`. `25` pages contained text and `4` pages returned no text: `T5-p014`, `T5-p015`, `T5-p027`, and `T5-p029`.

events.summary: Completed the full `tesseract` pass on `T5` with warnings logged.
events.details: Ran `tesseract` with witness-local `chi_tra` traineddata across all 29 `T5` images and saved `.txt` outputs under `ocr/T5/ocr/tesseract-full-pass/`. The run completed without page failures, but `28` pages emitted scale warnings on very small fragments.

events.summary: Completed the full `PaddleOCR PP-OCRv4` pass on `T5` and derived usable text support.
events.details: Ran the established machine-local `PP-OCRv4` path across all 29 `T5` images and saved page-level JSON outputs under `ocr/T5/ocr/paddleocr-ppocrv4/`. As on `T1` and `T4`, the recoverable text layer was derived from `res.rec_texts` into `ocr/T5/ocr/paddleocr-ppocrv4/extracted-text/` because the raw saved Paddle `.txt` companions are not the trustworthy text layer on this machine.

events.summary: Completed the full `EasyOCR` pass on `T5` after normalizing the serializer path.
events.details: The first `EasyOCR` save attempt failed on NumPy `int32` serialization before any page outputs landed. The normalized serializer was then used for a successful full pass across all 29 `T5` images under `ocr/T5/ocr/easyocr-full-pass/`. `28` pages contained text and only `T5-p029` was empty.

events.summary: Recorded `T5` as full-pass compliant across all four OCR engines.
events.details: The package now records `T5` as having full-pass `RapidOCR`, `tesseract`, `PaddleOCR PP-OCRv4`, and `EasyOCR` coverage on disk, with the target body span isolated to `T5-p004` through `T5-p006`. `T5` is no longer a queued comparison control; it is ready for bounded comparison use.

events.summary: Restored the corrupted live `T1-p032.l01` line to the already logged accepted reading.
events.details: The correction log, unresolved register, and comparison table already agreed on `ä¸€å¿ƒä¸ç”Ÿè¬æ³•ç„¡å’Ž` at `T1-p032.l01`, but the working file had drifted into eight literal question marks. The live transcription was repaired to match the already accepted package state before further comparison conclusions were recorded.

events.summary: Completed the first bounded `T5` confirmation slice against the recovered `T1` lemma spine.
events.details: Direct image review of `T5-p004` through `T5-p006`, checked against the saved four-engine OCR stack, broadly confirms the recovered `T1` poem spine from opening through close, including the already resolved `T4`-supported repairs. This slice exposed no new high-confidence repair locus and leaves `T5` as corroborative comparison support rather than a source of fresh forced changes.

events.summary: Opened `T2` from the locked NDL PDF package and completed its page-role pass.
events.details: Rendered the `28`-page `Sengcan_Faith_in_Mind_NDL_Shibulu/NDL2537640_shiburoku.pdf` witness into in-package `ocr/T2/page-images/`, created `ocr/T2/metadata.json`, and classified `T2-p003` through `T2-p005` as the target `Faith in Mind` body span. `T2-p001` and `T2-p002` are wrapper or title matter, `T2-p006` is immediate post-text colophon or imprint matter, `T2-p007` through `T2-p027` are non-target anthology material, and `T2-p028` is rear wrapper matter.

events.summary: Completed the first full `RapidOCR` pass on `T2` using explicitly logged OCR-input derivatives.
events.details: The full-resolution rendered `T2` PNG pages exceeded PIL decompression-bomb limits on this workstation before `RapidOCR` could begin, so a separate `ocr/T2/ocr-input-120dpi/` JPEG derivative set was created and logged as the engine input basis. `RapidOCR` then processed all `28` pages successfully, returned text on all `28`, and recorded no errors.

events.summary: Completed the full `tesseract` pass on `T2` using the same logged OCR-input derivatives.
events.details: The `T2` `tesseract` pass used the same `ocr/T2/ocr-input-120dpi/` derivative basis already logged for `RapidOCR`, together with the witness-local `chi_tra` model path under `ocr/T1/tessdata/`. The run exceeded shell timeouts during the first long attempt and had to be resumed, but the saved outputs now cover all `28` pages. All `28` pages returned text and only `T2-p027` emitted the familiar tiny-fragment scale warnings.

events.summary: Adopted evaluation-driven authority, evidence-strength, and stage-separation safeguards for the package.
events.details: After reading `critical_edition_evaluation.md`, the package explicitly adopted three tightening rules: `current-state.md` is the authoritative resumability surface when package logs disagree, non-trivial editorial actions must carry explicit evidence basis plus evidence strength, and recon / transcription / collation / edition stages must not be mixed casually. The governing repo docs and this package decision layer were updated before further `T2` OCR-compliance work continued.

events.summary: Completed the full `PaddleOCR PP-OCRv4` pass on `T2` and derived the usable text layer.
events.details: The `T2` Paddle pass used the same logged `ocr/T2/ocr-input-120dpi/` derivative basis already established for the other OCR engines. All `28` pages completed successfully under `ocr/T2/ocr/paddleocr-ppocrv4/`. As on earlier witnesses, the raw saved Paddle `.txt` companions were not the stable text surface on this machine, so the recoverable text was derived from saved JSON into `ocr/T2/ocr/paddleocr-ppocrv4/extracted-text/`.

events.summary: Completed the full `EasyOCR` pass on `T2` using the same logged OCR-input derivatives.
events.details: The `T2` EasyOCR pass used the same `ocr/T2/ocr-input-120dpi/` derivative basis already logged for the other engines. The first long run exceeded the shell timeout and had to be resumed, but the saved outputs now cover all `28` pages under `ocr/T2/ocr/easyocr-full-pass/`. All `28` pages returned text and no saved engine errors remain.

events.summary: Completed the first bounded `T2` confirmation slice against the recovered `T1` lemma spine.
events.details: Direct image review of `T2-p003` through `T2-p005`, checked against the saved four-engine OCR stack on the explicitly logged `ocr-input-120dpi/` derivative basis, broadly confirms the recovered `T1` poem spine from opening through close, including the already resolved `T4`-supported repairs. This slice exposed no new high-confidence repair locus and leaves `T2` as corroborative comparison support rather than a source of fresh forced changes.

events.summary: Opened `T3` as the next comparison-control witness and classified its late anthology body span.
events.details: The locked Kyoto `入衆日用` witness under `Faith_in_Mind_Sengcan_Kyoto_RB00016909` has now been opened in-package with `ocr/T3/metadata.json` and `ocr/T3/page-map.csv`. Visual review showed that the actual `Faith in Mind` body is delayed inside the anthology and occupies only `T3-p034` through `T3-p036`, with wrapper or title matter on `T3-p001` to `T3-p003` and `T3-p040` to `T3-p041`.

events.summary: Completed full four-engine OCR coverage on `T3` and derived the usable Paddle support layer.
events.details: The `T3` witness now has full-pass saved outputs from `RapidOCR`, `tesseract`, `PaddleOCR PP-OCRv4`, and `EasyOCR`. `RapidOCR` returned text on `39` of `41` pages, `EasyOCR` on `40` of `41`, and `tesseract` on all `41` while still emitting the familiar small-fragment scale warnings. The usable Paddle support was derived from saved JSON into `ocr/T3/ocr/paddleocr-ppocrv4/extracted-text/` because the recoverable text was stored under nested `res.rec_texts` and the raw saved `.txt` companions were not the stable text surface on this machine.

events.summary: Completed the first bounded `T3` confirmation slice against the recovered `T1` lemma spine.
events.details: Direct image review of `T3-p034` through `T3-p036`, checked against the saved four-engine OCR stack on the source-image basis, broadly confirms the recovered `T1` poem spine from opening through close and especially strengthens the already logged omission judgment at `T1-p075` because `T3` clearly preserves the standard `若不如此必不須守` line before `若如是必不須守`. This slice exposed no new high-confidence repair locus beyond what `T4` had already justified.

events.summary: Opened `A1` from the locked Waseda PDF package and classified its early anthology body span.
events.details: The locked Waseda `Shiburoku` witness under `Faith_in_Mind_Waseda_Commons/WUL-bunko31_e1087_shiburoku.pdf` has now been rendered in-package with `ocr/A1/page-images/`, `ocr/A1/metadata.json`, and `ocr/A1/page-map.csv`. Visual review showed that the actual `Faith in Mind` body occupies only `A1-p003` through `A1-p008`, with wrapper or title matter on `A1-p001` to `A1-p002`, later anthology material on `A1-p009` to `A1-p041`, and rear wrapper or closing tail matter on `A1-p042`.

events.summary: Completed full four-engine OCR coverage on `A1` and derived the usable Paddle support layer.
events.details: The `A1` witness now has full-pass saved outputs from `RapidOCR`, `tesseract`, `PaddleOCR PP-OCRv4`, and `EasyOCR`. `RapidOCR` returned text on `37` of `42` pages and left `A1-p022`, `A1-p025`, `A1-p028`, `A1-p036`, and `A1-p042` empty, while `EasyOCR` returned text on `41` of `42` pages and left only `A1-p042` empty. `tesseract` returned text on all `42` pages while still emitting the familiar small-fragment scale warnings. The usable Paddle support was derived from saved JSON into `ocr/A1/ocr/paddleocr-ppocrv4/extracted-text/` because the raw saved `.txt` companions were not the stable text surface on this machine.

events.summary: Completed the first bounded `A1` confirmation slice against the recovered `T1` lemma spine.
events.details: Direct image review of `A1-p003` through `A1-p006`, with `A1-p007` through `A1-p008` treated as immediate post-close or appended explanatory matter on the same target leaves, broadly confirms the recovered `T1` poem spine from opening through close. `A1` especially strengthens the already logged omission judgment at `T1-p075` because `A1-p006` clearly preserves the standard closing line `若不如是必不須守` before visible `若如是必不須守`. This slice exposed no new high-confidence repair locus beyond what `T4` had already justified.

events.summary: Opened `A2` from the locked Waseda derivative PDF package and classified its long body span.
events.details: The locked Waseda `四部録抄` witness under `Faith_in_Mind_Waseda_Commons/WUL-bunko31_e1089_shiburokushou.pdf` has now been rendered in-package with `ocr/A2/page-images/`, `ocr/A2/metadata.json`, and `ocr/A2/page-map.csv`. Visual review showed that the derivative body begins immediately on `A2-p002` and continues through `A2-p044`, with front wrapper matter on `A2-p001`, colophon-like tail matter on `A2-p045`, and rear wrapper matter on `A2-p046`.

events.summary: Completed full four-engine OCR coverage on `A2` and derived the usable Paddle support layer.
events.details: The `A2` witness now has full-pass saved outputs from `RapidOCR`, `tesseract`, `PaddleOCR PP-OCRv4`, and `EasyOCR`. The final clean `RapidOCR` rerun returned text on `44` of `46` pages and left only `A2-p045` and `A2-p046` empty after an earlier timed-out summary write was superseded. `tesseract` returned text on all `46` pages while still emitting the familiar small-fragment scale warnings. `PaddleOCR PP-OCRv4` completed across all `46` pages, and the usable Paddle support was derived from saved JSON into `ocr/A2/ocr/paddleocr-ppocrv4/extracted-text/` because the raw saved `.txt` companions were not the stable text surface on this machine. `EasyOCR` completed after a resumable two-pass closeout and returned text on `45` of `46` pages, with only `A2-p046` recorded as no-text in the saved summary.

events.summary: Completed the first bounded representative `A2` confirmation slice against the recovered `T1` lemma spine.
